seo.title: Kling 3 Standard | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Kling 3 Standard on MaxVideoAI for lower-cost multi-shot AI video, native audio, and live specs and pricing before you render.

overview: Kling 3 Standard is the current lower-cost Kling AI model for multi-shot prompts, start-frame Kling image-to-video runs, reusable Kling Elements, optional end frames, and native audio draft workflows.
pricingNotes: Member display estimates include the MaxVideoAI margin: about $0.16/s with audio, $0.11/s audio off, and $0.20/s with voice control.

hero.title: Kling 3 Standard — current Kling variant for lower-cost multi-shot video
hero.intro: Use Kling 3 Standard when you want the current lower-cost Kling AI model for multi-shot video, start-frame Kling image-to-video tests, reusable Kling Elements, optional end frames, and native audio before moving selected jobs into Kling 3 Pro. Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard when images should guide the shot without becoming the first frame.

technicalOverview.label: Details
technicalOverview.body: Kling 3 Standard is built for structured generation: multi-shot prompts, reusable Kling Elements (@Element1…), optional end frame, and native audio with optional voice IDs. Use it when you want storyboard-style control and lots of variants at a more cost-effective tier.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video; Image → Video (PNG/JPG/WebP), optional end frame

technicalOverview.label: Controls
technicalOverview.body: Multi-prompt scenes, Kling Elements, shot type, voice IDs

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: Native audio on by default; toggle off or enable voice control

promptStructure.title: Prompting Kling 3 Standard
promptStructure.quote: Scene 1: [shot]. Scene 2: [shot]. Scene 3: [shot]. Set a duration for each scene.
promptStructure.description: Break a clip into scenes, then anchor continuity with Kling Elements and an end frame.
steps: Decide on audio or voice IDs

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use elements to keep style and characters aligned.
Voice control is optional and adds a small fee.

prompts.title: Travel cut with three scenes
prompts.prompt: Scene 1 (4s): sunrise over a foggy ridge. Scene 2 (6s): hikers crossing a suspension bridge. Scene 3 (4s): close-up of a camera capturing golden hour portraits.

prompts.title: Product hero beats
prompts.prompt: Scene 1 (5s): macro of a luxury watch face. Scene 2 (4s): rotating hero shot with rim light. Scene 3 (4s): wrist-on model, soft ambience.

faqs.a: Yes. You can split a clip into multiple scenes with separate prompts.
Kling Elements let you define characters or props once, then reuse them across shots to keep identity and object continuity steadier in the same clip.

faqs.q: Is Kling 3 Standard a good Kling AI model for image-to-video prompt testing?
faqs.a: Yes, when the uploaded image should be the visible start frame. Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard for lower-cost reference-to-video tests where storyboard images guide the shot without opening the clip.

faqs.q: What is the difference vs Pro?
faqs.a: Standard includes multi-prompt structure, Elements, native audio, lip sync and voice IDs at a lower rate. Pro is the stronger pass for fidelity, stability and final-quality approvals.

heroHighlights: Structured multi-shot generation||Break one clip into multiple prompts and timing beats for storyboard-first control
Reusable Kling Elements for consistency||Define characters/props once, then reference them as @Element1 / @Element2 to reduce drift across shots
Native audio when you want it||Generate dialogue + ambience + SFX in the same pass, or toggle audio off for silent drafts
End-frame control for cleaner finishes||Use an optional end frame to land transitions, match cuts, and product reveals more predictably
custom.specTitle: Real Specs – Kling 3 Standard in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: Specs reflect the active MaxVideoAI route today.

specSections.title: Storyboard-ready multi-shot
specSections.intro: Split a clip into timed beats for structured shots at a cost-efficient tier.
items: Best with 2–4 shots and clear timestamps.
One action per shot for coherence.

specSections.title: Elements + audio flexibility
specSections.intro: Keep characters/props consistent and decide whether to render sound.
items: Optional end frame for tidy finishes.
Native audio on/off, voice IDs supported.
Great for ads, promos, and variants.

custom.tipsIntro: Kling 3 Standard is most predictable when you plan it like a storyboard: simple shots, consistent elements, and short dialogue.
strengths: 3–15s clips with 2–4 shots and clear timestamps/shot labels.
Use Elements for characters/props you want to keep stable.
For audio: one short line + ambience + 1 key SFX (keep it minimal).
Use an end frame when you need a clean landing or match cut.
troubleshootingItems: Drift across shots → repeat anchors + use @Element references; simplify each shot to one action.
Camera feels chaotic → one move per shot; avoid “dynamic” wording; specify “smooth track” or “tripod-stable”.
Dialogue/lip sync drifts → shorten lines; reduce fast head turns; keep the shot calmer.
Random text/logos → strengthen negative (“no text, no logos, no UI”) and keep signage out of frame.
boundaries: Short-form only (up to 15s); stitch for longer narratives.
Voice IDs are limited (max 2) and audio language behavior depends on routing.
End frame is optional and works best when the final composition is clearly described.

custom.safetyNote: Built-in safeguards and best practices for responsible creation with Kling 3 Standard.

decisionCards.title: Standard or Pro?
decisionCards.body: Use Standard for lower-cost start-frame testing. Use Kling 3 Pro when the approved shot needs stronger final fidelity.

decisionCards.title: Planning multiple shots?
decisionCards.body: Keep each beat short, assign one action per shot, and reuse continuity anchors across the sequence.

decisionCards.title: Need final delivery?
decisionCards.body: Validate in Standard first, then move approved shots into Pro when fidelity, stability and final-quality approval matter more.

referenceWorkflows.title: Shot plan

section.title: How to Write a Great Kling 3 Standard Prompt
section.intro: Kling 3 Standard performs best with shot-based direction: clear framing, one action per shot, explicit motion, and (if audio is on) short dialogue plus minimal sound cues.
section.referencesTitle: How Kling 3 Standard structures shots and references

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.
tabs.copy:
[One subject] [one visible action] in [setting], [framing + one camera move], [lighting/style].
Audio (optional): [ambience + 1 SFX cue OR one short line].
Negative: no text, no logos, no subtitles/overlays.

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
tabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
tabs.copy:
Scene anchors:
[Subject + wardrobe/props + 2–3 environment anchors.]

Shots (multi-shot):
Shot 1 (0–5s): [framing + action + one camera move]
Shot 2 (5–10s): [framing + action + one camera move]
Shot 3 (10–15s): [final action/reveal + settle or end frame]

Elements (optional):
@Element1 = [main character]
@Element2 = [prop/product]

Audio (optional):
Dialogue: <<<voice_1>>> “Short line.”
Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues. Keep it minimal.

Constraints:
No logos. No readable text. No subtitles/overlays.

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "film crew brief")
tabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific cinematic look or continuity across shots.
tabs.copy:
Director brief:
[Goal + vibe + intended audience.]

Continuity (repeat across shots):
- Subject identity + wardrobe/props
- Location + time + 3 visual anchors
- Lighting + palette anchors
- Lens feel + one camera move per shot

Multi-shot plan (up to 15s total):
Shot 1: [framing + action + camera]
Shot 2: [framing + action + camera]
Shot 3: [framing + action + camera]
(optional) Shot 4–6: [only if needed]

Elements:
@Element1 = [character]
@Element2 = [product/prop]

Audio (if on):
Dialogue: <<<voice_1>>> “One short line.”
Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues + optional music vibe.

Negative:
No text, no logos, no subtitles/overlays, no jump cuts.

tabs.label: Storyboard
tabs.title: Storyboard prompt (multi-shot / shot list)
tabs.description: Use this when you want a mini-story in one clip. A storyboard prompt (aka multi-shot / shot list prompt) gives clear timing, camera direction, and continuity. Also called shot-list or sequenced prompts.
tabs.copy:
Duration: [3–15s] • Aspect: [16:9 / 9:16 / 1:1] • Audio: [on/off]

Elements (optional):
@Element1 = [main character/subject]
@Element2 = [prop/product]

Shot 1 (0–3s):
[Framing + action + camera move]

Shot 2 (3–8s):
[Framing + action + camera move]

Shot 3 (8–15s):
[Final action/reveal + camera settle]
End frame (optional): [describe the final frame you want to land on]

Audio (if on):
Dialogue: <<<voice_1>>> “Short line.”
Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues.

Constraints:
No logos. No readable text. No subtitles/overlays. Keep it to one action per shot.

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = control. Use for precise, repeatable looks.
Storyboard = shot timing. Use for multi-shot beats.

globalPrinciples: Think in shots, not clips: one action per shot is easier to execute cleanly.
Anchor your subjects early (and reuse the same wording) to reduce drift across shots.
Put framing + camera movement before style adjectives.
If audio is on: keep dialogue short and add only 1–2 sound cues.
engineWhy: Multi-shot works best with 2–4 shots; keep each shot to one readable action.
Elements improve continuity: reference @Element1/@Element2 consistently across the prompt.
Voice IDs: reference voices in the prompt as <<<voice_1>>> and <<<voice_2>>> (max 2 voices).
Audio output supports English and Chinese; other languages may be auto-translated to English when audio is enabled.
End frame helps land transitions; avoid adding new actions in the final second.

demo.title: Demo prompt — Kling 3 Standard
demo.prompt:
Duration: 12s • Aspect: 16:9 • Audio: on
Shot 1 (0-4s): @Element1 runner in a dark jacket waits under a neon sign, light rain, stable medium shot.
Shot 2 (4-8s): @Element1 crosses the street, smooth side tracking, blue and red reflections on wet pavement.
Shot 3 (8-12s): he slows beside a storefront, camera settles, same wardrobe and lighting anchors.
Audio: wet street ambience, short footsteps, light breath. No text, no logos, no subtitles.

summary.subject: Runner on a neon street
summary.action: Three short beats move from launch to crossing and final settle
summary.camera: 1080p storyboard shots, stable tracking and readable cuts
summary.style: Rainy night, neon reflections, social promo look
summary.output: Audio on: street ambience and one short footstep cue
